test.name: Review existing plan and confirm next plan item
test.description: Plan pl_002 already exists for q_001 with pli_004 (1860 census, completed), pli_005 (death cert, completed), and pli_006 (probate, in_progress). The skill should recognize the plan is mostly executed and confirm that the probate item is the logical next step, not propose a new parallel plan. Tests the review-vs-create distinction.

input.user_message: Create or review the research plan for the parentage question.

additional_criteria: Should NOT create a new `pl_` plan when pl_002 already exists for q_001 and is `active` — research-plan should review the existing plan, not produce a parallel one (which would supersede pl_002 unnecessarily)

Should confirm pli_006 (probate, in_progress) is the right next step: probate records may name Patrick as son of Thomas, providing direct positive evidence for parentage that the census records currently establish only indirectly
Should consult the collections-schuylkill fixture to verify probate records for Schuylkill County 1870-1890 are actually available (and through which collection / repository)
Should NOT mark pli_006 as completed if it isn't actually executed — research-plan reviews the plan; the in_progress status reflects what's actually happened in the log
Should mention what would follow probate: post-1860 censuses (1870, 1880, 1900) to track Patrick into adulthood and establish residence continuity
